5 Indonesian Industries at Highest Risk for Arc Flash Incidents (And What to Do)

Arc flash accidents occur within seconds but result in lifetime injuries. Arc flash explosions are hotter than the surface of the sun and can permanently injure or kill employees. Most Indonesian facilities are unaware of just how hazardous their workplaces actually are. If you are employed in these five industries, you must be aware of your exposures and know what to do.

The 5 Highest Risk Industries in Indonesia

  • 1. Oil and Gas Refineries: Oil and gas plants are like ticking time bombs when it comes to electrical safety. You've got 24/7 high-voltage equipment, alongside flammable materials. A little electrical explosion can escalate into a catastrophe. Hence, the risk of arc flash incidents is higher.
  • What to do:
  • Obtain a professional arc flash risk assessment in Indonesia
  • Put proper warning signs on all electrical equipment
  • Train employees in emergency procedures
  • Utilize the appropriate Arc Flash PPE for every location
  • 2. Mining Operations: Indonesian mines are exposed to special electrical hazards. Heavy equipment, dust, and harsh working conditions increase electrical accidents. Underground mines are particularly dangerous due to the possibility that help may be distant.
  • What you need to do:
  • Periodic inspections of electrical equipment
  • Correct Arc Flash Boundary labeling around all equipment
  • Emergency response training for off-site areas
  • Weather-resistant electrical protection
  • 3. Palm Oil Processing Plants : Palm oil processing uses strong motors and electrical equipment in hot, humid environments. Electrical issues love the combination of electricity and humidity. These Facilities have a lot of older equipment that does not meet current safety expectations.
  • What you need to do:
  • Climate-appropriate electrical enclosures
  • Frequent Moisture and corrosion inspections
  • Good ventilation for electrical areas
  • Arc Flash Suit requirements for maintenance
  • 4. Textile and Garment Manufacturing : Textile mills may appear safer than heavy industry, but they also present their electrical hazards. Older structures tend to have obsolete electrical systems, and lint from cloth can lead to fire dangers close to electrical equipment.
  • What you need to do:
  • Replace aging electrical panels and wiring
  • Clean regularly around electrical equipment
  • Correct arc flash protection training for maintenance personnel
  • Clear electrical maintenance procedures
  • 5. Food and Beverage Manufacturing : Food factories possess a lethal mix: water everywhere and a lot of electrical equipment. Cleaning operations tend to bring water close to electrical equipment, generating additional hazards for employees.
  • What you need to do:
  • Water-resistant electrical installations
  • Lockout/tagout practices during cleaning
  • Ground fault protection on every circuit
  • Routine training in wet-weather safety

How Care Labs Indonesia Can Help Protect Your Workers

At Care Labs Indonesia, we know each facility is unique. Our services for arc flash studies are tailored to Indonesian industry conditions. We don’t simply deliver a report – we provide you with workable solutions applicable in actual situations.
  • Our Expert Arc Flash Study Includes :
  • Total facility electrical analysis
  • Arc flash hazard assessment in Indonesia according to local conditions
  • Special safety procedure design for your particular industry
  • Training sessions for your maintenance staff
  • Continued assistance to ensure your employees are protected

Take Action Now - Your Workers' Lives Depend on It

Don’t wait until an accident occurs. Arc Flash protection in Indonesia is not merely about complying with regulations – it’s about ensuring your employees get home safely every day. The expense of doing it is very little compared to the expense of an accident.
Begin by having your facility professionally evaluated. Have knowledge of your hazards, train your staff, and ensure your staff is equipped with the proper protective gear. Your employees rely on you to protect them; ensure you’re doing all that you can.

Ready to protect your team? Contact Care Labs Indonesia today for a comprehensive arc flash assessment.

High Electricity Bills Crushing Your Indonesian Business? Load Flow Analysis Can Cut Costs!

The high energy intensity and fluctuating electricity tariffs negatively impact the energy costs in many Indonesian industrial facilities. Many facility owners and operation managers find it difficult to control the electricity bills, a substantial weight that drags down business goals. If you are a concerned person, you might have even tried shutting down idle electrical equipment, and still find the electricity costs rising.
Have you ever considered that this can be due to the power flow through the network rather than power usage?
Here is where the game-changer analysis technique called load flow analysis can help you! It can be described as a method of power system analysis that identifies the inefficiencies in the system. The recommendations and reports give a fair idea of how to rectify the anomalies in the system.

Load Flow Analysis Explained

As a power system consultant, Care Labs helps industries align their operational goals with the power system efficiency. Many analysis techniques like load flow, short circuit analysis, arc flash study, and similar techniques, help businesses make their electrical system safe, compliant, and reliable to use. With each analysis offering different insights, load flow analysis identifies the area where there are energy losses in the system.
During a load flow analysis, Care Labs uses ETAP software to identify the current, voltage, active, and reactive power available in the network. Using this tool, a digital twin of your system is created, and the following parameters are checked:
  • Where voltage drops occur
  • Which equipment is overloaded
  • Power is lost in transmission and distribution
  • Is the system operating efficiently
For Indonesian industries, the data from power flow analysis is a bonanza for power optimization.

The Hidden Cost Of Optimized Power

Since most of the electrical issues and potential hazards are hidden, we need to model them to analyze the details. Overloaded feeders may cause energy losses, power may be unevenly distributed in the system, and voltage drops at various points can increase the current drawn at various points. A load flow analysis helps uncover these hidden challenges in the system.

How Load Flow Analysis Helps Cut Energy Costs?

A well-performed load flow analysis can help cut the bills by:
  • Reducing power losses and energy wastage
  • Improving power factors through capacitor banks and filters.
  • Reducing the operational costs by preventing the oversizing of equipment.
  • Preventing costly downtime and equipment damage.

Why This Matters In Indonesia?

The electrical infrastructure here faces unique challenges like voltage instability in expanding industrial zones, SNI, PLN standards compliance, and varying electricity tariffs. In such a scenario, what your facility needs is not just an efficient electrical system, but a compliant one as well. A load flow analysis in compliance with these standards is the right solution to overcome these discrepancies in the system.
To sum everything up, if you are facing exorbitant electricity bills in your facility in Indonesia, then the problem might not be just consumption. It can be load flow and energy losses. A Load flow analysis is the right solution to understand this and rectify the defects.
Ready to Take Control of Electricity Costs In Indonesia?
Contact Care Labs, Indonesia, today to schedule a customized load flow analysis that offers actionable guidance to save big on energy costs.

Nuisance Tripping Disrupting Indonesian Production? Relay Coordination Fixes It

Diverse industries form the foundation of Indonesia’s thriving economy. From manufacturing to real estate, a long list of industries contributes significantly to its prosperity. In this rapidly growing industrial landscape, even a single downtime event can cause millions in losses. What’s often to blame in situations like this? It can be a lack of proper relay coordination. A well-designed and implemented protection strategy can reduce these nuisance trippings and ensure operational continuity.

Care Labs is a trusted engineering partner specializing in the study and analysis of electrical power systems. We combine global tools with local compliance to make your electrical systems efficient, reliable, and compliant with regulations.

What Is Nuissance Tripping & Why Is It Important?

When a protective relay, due to a lack of coordination, trips and disrupts the operation, it’s called nuisance tripping. The effect of this can be summarized as:
  • Unnecessary shutdown of non-faulty equipment.
  • Chain reactions across unrelated electrical zones.
  • Loss of operational hours
  • Strain on maintenance teams and operational confidence
  • Costly repairs
In Indonesia, where industrial growth and grid conditions are dynamic, nuisance trippings can be costly and risky, yet preventable.

Relay Coordination Is Your First Line Of Defense

The engineering technique used to analyze and identify the settings and coordination of these protective devices, like relays, switchgears, and circuit breakers, is called a relay coordination study. When these protective devices are working in a time-based logical sequence, any faults in the system can be isolated without disrupting the entire system. They work as follows:

  • The protective device, like the relay closest to the fault, trips first.
  • Other equipment remains intact, and the fault is prevented from escalating.
  • With minimal impact, quick recovery is ensured.

What causes defects in Relay Coordination?

In Indonesia, the root cause of nuisance tripping and defective relay coordination can be summarized as:

  • The default relay settings were not corrected
  • Lack of regular power system studies during expansion/upgrading.
  • Use of multiple OEM relays that are not synced.
  • Absence of coordination studies during commissioning.

What Happens During A Relay Coordination Study

A professional relay coordination study includes the following:

  • Full modeling of the electrical system, including transformers, relays, switches, and so on.
  • Identifying the fault current levels and simulating these faults.
  • Time Current Characteristic graph plotting and analysis.
  • Fine-tuning the coordination by adjusting the currents and time delays.
  • Report and recommendations, fully aligned with IEEE, IEC, and Indonesian standards like PLN, & SNI.
Don’t let small trips affect your business in Indonesia. Let’s coordinate your relays and ensure stability in operations.

How Short Circuit Analysis Protects Manufacturing Units in Indonesia from Deadly Fires?

A Safety Gap Most Manufacturers Neglect

In Indonesia’s rapidly expanding manufacturing industry, safety measures tend to concentrate on fire alarm systems, fire extinguishers, and practicing emergency drills. But what if the actual danger is from within the electric system itself, where a fault can incite a fire in a matter of seconds, well before any alarm signal?
That’s where short circuit analysis helps— Essentially, it detects faults before they cause any major harm.

Fire Doesn't Begin Large — It Begins Small

In Manufacturing units, electrical malfunctions can accumulate silently. A frayed cable, a compromised joint, or an overloaded panel can appear trivial until it triggers a huge short circuit. The consequence? Unstoppable heat, machinery damage, and, in some cases, a massive fire.
Short circuit analysis helps locate these weak points early on. It shows where fault currents are likely to occur and just how bad they might be, enabling manufacturers to act before harm is caused.

Why Manufacturing Units In Indonesia Can’t Afford Skipping Short Circuit Analysis?

Indonesian manufacturing plants are growing rapidly, with increased demand and extended cycles of operation. Expansion frequently places additional stress on aging electrical systems, which introduces new fault hazards.
  • Understanding their system's existing limits
  • Emphasizing weak points
  • Preventing accidents due to electrical overloads
Therefore, short-circuit analysis isn’t merely about compliance — it’s safeguarding operations, equipment, and personnel.

What’s at Risk When Short Circuit Analysis is Ignored?

Without short-circuit analysis, a facility will not know how much current its system can carry in the event of a fault. And, ignoring short-circuit analysis could put your facility at risk:
  • Protection devices could fail to trip on time
  • Faults could propagate before they're contained.
  • Critical equipment might get damaged.
Even Worse, the whole electrical system could shut down, stopping production and risking fires.

Why Top Industries Opt for Care Labs

We collaborate with manufacturing teams to render their electrical systems safer and more dependable. Be it a new facility or an old one, we offer:
Don’t wait for a fault to reveal the flaws. Choose Care Labs and power up your protection strategy.

Short Circuit Analysis
How Short Circuit Analysis Protects Manufacturing Units in Indonesia from Deadly Fires?
A Safety Gap Most Manufacturers Neglect
In Indonesia’s rapidly expanding manufacturing industry, safety measures tend to concentrate on fire alarm systems, fire extinguishers, and practicing emergency drills. But what if the actual danger is from within the electric system itself, where a fault can incite a fire in a matter of seconds, well before any alarm signal?
That’s where short circuit analysis helps— Essentially, it detects faults before they cause any major harm.
Fire Doesn't Begin Large — It Begins Small
In Manufacturing units, electrical malfunctions can accumulate silently. A frayed cable, a compromised joint, or an overloaded panel can appear trivial until it triggers a huge short circuit. The consequence? Unstoppable heat, machinery damage, and, in some cases, a massive fire.
Short circuit analysis helps locate these weak points early on. It shows where fault currents are likely to occur and just how bad they might be, enabling manufacturers to act before harm is caused.
Why Manufacturing Units In Indonesia Can’t Afford Skipping Short Circuit Analysis?
Indonesian manufacturing plants are growing rapidly, with increased demand and extended cycles of operation. Expansion frequently places additional stress on aging electrical systems, which introduces new fault hazards.
Understanding their system's existing limits
Emphasizing weak points
Preventing accidents due to electrical overloads
Therefore, short-circuit analysis isn’t merely about compliance — it’s safeguarding operations, equipment, and personnel.
What’s at Risk When Short Circuit Analysis is Ignored?
Without short-circuit analysis, a facility will not know how much current its system can carry in the event of a fault. And, ignoring short-circuit analysis could put your facility at risk:
Protection devices could fail to trip on time
Faults could propagate before they're contained.
Critical equipment might get damaged.
Even Worse, the whole electrical system could shut down, stopping production and risking fires.
Why Top Industries Opt for Care Labs
We collaborate with manufacturing teams to render their electrical systems safer and more dependable. Be it a new facility or an old one, we offer:
Don’t wait for a fault to reveal the flaws. Choose Care Labs and power up your protection strategy.
